title: Henri Pille

infobox.value: (4 January 1844 – 4 March 1897) was a French painter and illustrator.
Mémoires de la Société historique et archéologique de Château-Thierry, tome 44, p.247 [[1]](http://www.histoireaisne.fr/memoires_numerises/chapitres/tome_44/Tome_044_page_245.pdf)

Silhouette en zinc : *personnage plongeant*, exécuté pour "les Oies de Javotte" [[2]](http://www.photo.rmn.fr/cf/htm/CPicZ.aspx?E=2C6NU0IW28BR) [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20140222065831/http://www.photo.rmn.fr/cf/htm/CPicZ.aspx?E=2C6NU0IW28BR) 2014-02-22 at the [Wayback Machine](/wiki/Wayback_Machine "Wayback Machine")

Vincent van Gogh *The letters*, éditées par le Van Gogh Museum et le Huygens Institute (Pour ouvrir ces pages, les cookies doivent êre activés) : [n°162](http://vangoghletters.org/vg/letters/let162/letter.html#translation), [n°234](http://vangoghletters.org/vg/letters/let234/letter.html#translation), [n°318](http://vangoghletters.org/vg/letters/let318/letter.html#translation), [n°333](http://vangoghletters.org/vg/letters/let333/letter.html#translation), [n°494](http://vangoghletters.org/vg/letters/let494/letter.html#translation), [n°496](http://vangoghletters.org/vg/letters/let496/letter.html#translation).

*Don Quichotte de la Manche*, numérisé par le Center for the Study of Digital Libraries [[3]](http://www.csdl.tamu.edu:8080/dqiDisplayInterface/doSearchImages.jsp?id=183&orderBy=1&page=1) [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20140222194354/http://www.csdl.tamu.edu:8080/dqiDisplayInterface/doSearchImages.jsp?id=183&orderBy=1&page=1) 2014-02-22 at the [Wayback Machine](/wiki/Wayback_Machine "Wayback Machine")
[Notice et reproduction de l'œuvre sur le site du musée](http://carnavalet.paris.fr/fr/collections/une-cantine-municipale-pendant-le-siege-de-paris-1870-1871)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

French painter and illustrator

| Charles Henri Pille | |
| --- | --- |
| Henri Pille in *Galerie contemporaine, littéraire et artistique*c. 1875 | |
| Born | (1844-01-04)4 January 1844  [Essômes-sur-Marne](/wiki/Ess%C3%B4mes-sur-Marne "Essômes-sur-Marne"), Aisne, France |
| Died | 4 March 1897(1897-03-04) (aged 53)  [Paris](/wiki/Paris "Paris"), France |
| Occupations | Painter and illustrator |

**Charles Henri Pille** (4 January 1844 – 4 March 1897) was a French painter and illustrator.

## Life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Henri_Pille&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Life")]

Charles Henri Pille was born in [Essômes-sur-Marne](/wiki/Ess%C3%B4mes-sur-Marne "Essômes-sur-Marne"), Aisne, on 4 January 1844.
He studied under [Félix-Joseph Barrias](/wiki/F%C3%A9lix-Joseph_Barrias "Félix-Joseph Barrias").
He submitted his first painting to the [Salon](/wiki/Salon_(Paris) "Salon (Paris)") in 1865.
In 1869 he received the Gold Medal of the Ministry of the Emperor's household and of Fine Arts.
He was awarded a gold medal at the [Paris Exposition of 1889](/wiki/Exposition_Universelle_(1889) "Exposition Universelle (1889)").

He was recognized by the artistic community of [Montmartre](/wiki/Montmartre "Montmartre").[[1]](#cite_note-1)
His work developed with historical scenes and genre scenes that were often tinged with humor. He is best known for his pen drawings.
He made numerous illustrations for publishers, particularly [Alphonse Lemerre](/wiki/Alphonse_Lemerre "Alphonse Lemerre").
He contributed to magazines and newspapers such as *[Le Courrier Français](/wiki/Le_Courrier_fran%C3%A7ais_(1884%E2%80%931913) "Le Courrier français (1884–1913)")*, *[Le Rire](/wiki/Le_Rire "Le Rire")*, *[Le Monde illustré](/wiki/Le_Monde_illustr%C3%A9 "Le Monde illustré")*, *Le Procope*, *[Journal amusant](/wiki/Journal_amusant "Journal amusant")*, *La Vie moderne*, *[Le Voleur](/wiki/Le_Voleur_(journal) "Le Voleur (journal)")* and *[Le Petit Français illustré](/wiki/Le_Petit_Fran%C3%A7ais_illustr%C3%A9 "Le Petit Français illustré")*.
He published drawings in the review of *[Le Chat Noir](/wiki/Le_Chat_Noir "Le Chat Noir")* cabaret, and helped design its silhouettes for its shadow theater shows.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

In his letters to his brother Théo, [Vincent van Gogh](/wiki/Vincent_van_Gogh "Vincent van Gogh") repeatedly expressed his admiration for the work of Henri Pille whom he met during his stay in Paris between May 1875 and March 1876.[[3]](#cite_note-3)
Henri Pille became a Knight of the [Legion of Honour](/wiki/Legion_of_Honour "Legion of Honour") in 1882.
He was president of the Society of Illustrators.
He died in Paris on 4 March 1897.

## Books illustrated by Henri Pille

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Henri_Pille&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Books illustrated by Henri Pille")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/6b/HenriPille-CantineMunicipaleSi%C3%A8geDeParis.JPG/250px-HenriPille-CantineMunicipaleSi%C3%A8geDeParis.JPG)](/wiki/File:HenriPille-CantineMunicipaleSi%C3%A8geDeParis.JPG)

Cantine municipale pendant le siège de Paris, 1870–1871, [musée Carnavalet](/wiki/Mus%C3%A9e_Carnavalet "Musée Carnavalet")

* *Complete Works of [Shakespeare](/wiki/William_Shakespeare "William Shakespeare")*, Paris, Alphonse Lemerre, 1875–1880
* *Works of [Alfred de Musset](/wiki/Alfred_de_Musset "Alfred de Musset")*, Paris, Alphonse Lemerre, 1876
* *Voyages en famille*, Joseph-Charles Vendryes, Paris, Ludovic Baschet, 1877
* *Les dames galantes*, [Brantôme](/wiki/Pierre_de_Bourdeille,_seigneur_de_Brant%C3%B4me "Pierre de Bourdeille, seigneur de Brantôme"), Paris, éditions Arnaud et Labat, 1879
* *Bleuette, Conte en vers*, [François Coppée](/wiki/Fran%C3%A7ois_Copp%C3%A9e "François Coppée"), Paris, Alphonse Lemerre, 1880
* *Le livre des convalescents*, [Ernest Alexandre Honoré Coquelin](/wiki/Ernest_Alexandre_Honor%C3%A9_Coquelin "Ernest Alexandre Honoré Coquelin") (under the pseudonym *Pirouette*), Paris, Tresse, 1880
* *Les Contes*, [Charles Perrault](/wiki/Charles_Perrault "Charles Perrault"), Paris, Alphonse Lemerre, 1880
* *Marie*, Paris, [Auguste Brizeux](/wiki/Auguste_Brizeux "Auguste Brizeux"), Alphonse Lemerre, 1881
* *Le Roman comique*, [Paul Scarron](/wiki/Paul_Scarron "Paul Scarron"), Paris, Alphonse Lemerre, 1881
* *Quentin Durward*, [Walter Scott](/wiki/Walter_Scott "Walter Scott"), Paris, Librairie de Firmin-Didot & Cie, 1881
* *Fariboles*, [Ernest Alexandre Honoré Coquelin](/wiki/Ernest_Alexandre_Honor%C3%A9_Coquelin "Ernest Alexandre Honoré Coquelin") (under the pseudonym *Pirouette*), Paris, Paul Ollendorf, 1882
* *Les Cinq Sous d'Isaac Laquedem – Le Juif Errant – Contes et histoires pour enfants*, Aimé Giron, Paris, Librairie Firmin-Didot, 1883.
* *Derniers contes bleus*, [Édouard Laboulaye](/wiki/%C3%89douard_Laboulaye "Édouard Laboulaye"), Paris, Jouvet et Cie, 1884
* *Selected works: Tales of the Merovingian times*, [Augustin Thierry](/wiki/Augustin_Thierry "Augustin Thierry"), Paris, Léon Bonhoure, 1885
* *Chansons et rondes enfantines*, [Jean-Baptiste Weckerlin](/wiki/Jean-Baptiste_Weckerlin "Jean-Baptiste Weckerlin"), Garnier Frères, 1885
* *Nouvelles Chansons et rondes enfantines*, Jean-Baptiste Weckerlin, Garnier Frères, 1886
* *II était une fois...*, Savinien Lapointe, Paris, Alphonse Lemerre, 1886
* *Notre-Dame de Paris*, [Victor Hugo](/wiki/Victor_Hugo "Victor Hugo"), Paris, Alphonse Lemerre, 1886
* *Histoire merveilleuse de Pierre Schlémihl, ou l'Homme qui a vendu son ombre*, [Adelbert von Chamisso](/wiki/Adelbert_von_Chamisso "Adelbert von Chamisso"), Paris, L. Westhausser, 1888.
* *Héros légendaires, leur véritable histoire*, Ernest d'Hervilly] Paris, Alphonse Lemerre, 1889
* *Pages d'autrefois*, Roger Miles, Paris A. Lanier & fils, 1889
* *Ma petite sœur Naïk*, Charles Delon, Paris, Alphonse Lemerre, 1891
* *Histoire du célèbre Pépé*, Edgar Monteil, Paris, Librairie de l'éducation de la jeunesse, 1891
* *Soldats de France, actions héroïques*, Gaston de Raimes, Paris, Alphonse Lemerre, 1892–1895
* *Les Maris de Colette*, Georges Bureau, Paris, Paul Ollendorff, 1895
* *La Souris blanche*, [Hégésippe Moreau](/wiki/H%C3%A9g%C3%A9sippe_Moreau "Hégésippe Moreau"), Paris, Pairault & Cie éditeurs, 1895
* *Les expédients de Farandole*, Pierre Perrault, Paris, Armand Colin, 1895[[4]](#cite_note-4)
* *Le Chevalier Carême*, Marc Guéchot, Paris, colin, 1896
* *Don Quichotte de la Manche*, [Miguel de Cervantes](/wiki/Miguel_de_Cervantes "Miguel de Cervantes"), Charavay, Mantoux et Martin, c. 1893[[5]](#cite_note-5)
* *Théâtre choisi*, [Molière](/wiki/Moli%C3%A8re "Molière"), preface by Léo Claretie, Paris, Charavay, Mantoux et Martin, c. 1900
* *Twenty Children's Pieces for Piano*, [Francis Thomé](/wiki/Francis_Thom%C3%A9 "Francis Thomé"), Henri Lemoine, undated

## Public Collections

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Henri_Pille&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Public Collections")]

* [The Louvre](/wiki/The_Louvre "The Louvre"), department of graphic arts
* [Musée d'Orsay](/wiki/Mus%C3%A9e_d%27Orsay "Musée d'Orsay")
* [Carnavalet Museum](/wiki/Carnavalet_Museum "Carnavalet Museum"), *Cantine municipale pendant le siège de Paris, 1870–1871*[[6]](#cite_note-6)
* National Museum of [Château de Pau](/wiki/Ch%C3%A2teau_de_Pau "Château de Pau"), drawing of the history of [Henry IV of France](/wiki/Henry_IV_of_France "Henry IV of France")[[7]](#cite_note-7)
* [Musée des Beaux-Arts de Nice](/wiki/Mus%C3%A9e_des_Beaux-Arts_de_Nice "Musée des Beaux-Arts de Nice"), *Puritains et cavaliers*
* [Museum of Fine Arts, Rheims](/wiki/Museum_of_Fine_Arts,_Rheims "Museum of Fine Arts, Rheims"), *La messe à Pavant (Aisne)*[[8]](#cite_note-8)
